summary: Comprehensive testing of SIGMA iteration 3 (RBAC + dynamic roles + user management). Backend: 70/70 pytest cases PASS across three test files (backend_test.py 27 regression + test_new_features.py 23 + test_rbac.py 20 new). All RBAC assertions verified: register 404 removed, login/me returns role_name+permissions+is_active, staff 200/403 enforcement (allowed: GET/POST assets, move, repairs CRUD; denied: DELETE asset, export, import, offices/rooms POST/PUT/DELETE, users, roles, permissions), permissions catalog returns 10 keys, roles CRUD with validation (invalid perm 422, empty perms 422, dup name 422, system role update/delete 422, role-with-users delete 422), users CRUD with duplicate email 422 / unknown role 422 / self-delete 422 / self-deactivate 422 / self-role-change 422 / deactivated login 403 + existing session /me 403 / reactivate works, LIVE role revocation confirmed (change to Viewer -> immediate 403 on POST /assets in same session without re-login). Frontend as Admin: Users page (add/edit/reset-password/deactivate/delete) fully functional, admin row 'Anda' badge + disabled toggle/delete verified, Roles page Sistem badge + Administrator edit/delete disabled verified. Frontend as Staff: sidebar hides Manajemen User + Role & Izin, no Import/Export on dashboard, no delete-trash icons, Tambah Aset visible, /users and /roles redirect to /, offices page has NO Tambah/edit/delete buttons, asset detail Perbaikan section visible.

issues: Sonner toasts stack from previous page in top-right and can intercept clicks on 'Tambah Role' if fired in quick succession. Cosmetic — dismissing/waiting resolves. Consider auto-dismissing toast on route change.

critical_code_review_comments: core.py get_current_user re-reads role.permissions from DB on EVERY authenticated request — makes role revocation instant (verified). Adds one extra query per request but acceptable for correctness.

core.py require_permission uses ANY-of-multiple semantics via any(p in user_perms for p in permissions) — routes /permissions and /roles use ('roles.manage','users.manage') so admins with either perm can view. Correct.
services.UserService self-protection is comprehensive: delete self 422, deactivate self 422, change own role 422. Verified.
services.RoleService.delete blocks system roles AND blocks roles with assigned users — good; matches spec.

seed.py._ensure_role always refreshes Administrator permissions on every startup ('is_system' branch) — good if the ALL_PERMISSION_KEYS catalog is extended later; Staff/Viewer permissions are NOT refreshed after first seed (might diverge from source-of-truth if code changes). Non-blocking; acceptable design if roles are meant to be user-editable.
routes.py login endpoint duplicates role_name+permissions enrichment logic from get_current_user — could be extracted into a helper for DRY. Minor.

routes.py POST/PUT/DELETE /users use require_permission('users.manage') — no 'roles.manage' fallback; an admin without users.manage but with roles.manage cannot manage users. Correct: separate concerns.

schemas.RoleIn.permissions has no explicit non-empty validator on the schema — empty list caught at service layer with 422 message 'Pilih minimal satu izin'. Could be moved to Field(min_length=1) for cleaner OpenAPI, but functionally correct.
UsersPage.jsx: password input in add form is required but on edit dialog the password field is not shown — reset password uses separate dialog. Good UX.

RolesPage.jsx: 'Sistem' badge appears twice inside the Administrator row (visible via count=2 in test) — one is the badge, likely the row+cell selector matches both a text on cell + badge. Cosmetic finding, not a bug.
AuthContext.jsx hasPerm helper properly uses stored user.permissions from /auth/me — permissions refresh on every /me call so navigation gating reflects DB.

AppLayout.jsx nav items filtered by hasPerm — verified: Manajemen User needs users.manage, Role & Izin needs roles.manage. Kantor/Ruangan visible to staff (assets.view) — spec says staff should NOT see edit/delete buttons but LIST is allowed. Verified matches spec.
App.js PermissionRoute redirects unauthorized users to / — verified for staff on /users and /roles.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py (updated TestAuth::test_login_success to assert role_name/permissions instead of legacy role field)
/app/backend/tests/test_rbac.py (created — 20 RBAC tests covering auth changes, staff enforcement, permissions catalog, roles CRUD, users CRUD, self-protection, live role revocation)
seed_data_creation: Test suites create/clean TEST_ prefixed roles, TEST-* assets, TEST User accounts. Playwright admin flow created + deleted 'TEST UI User' and 'TEST_UI_Role'. Live-role-revocation test created and cleaned a TEST-REV-* asset via admin session. No lingering test data.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Regression baseline is now 70 pytest cases across /app/backend/tests/{backend_test.py, test_new_features.py, test_rbac.py}. Login returns role_name (not 'role') and permissions[]. Public /api/auth/register is REMOVED (404). All admin RBAC endpoints require users.manage or roles.manage. GET /api/permissions and GET /api/roles accept EITHER users.manage OR roles.manage. Get_current_user re-reads DB on every request so role/permission changes are LIVE (no re-login needed). PermissionRoute in App.js gates client-side navigation. data-testids include colons/dots (e.g., perm-checkbox-assets.view) and hyphens with '&' (nav-role-&-izin) — Playwright handles these fine as attribute values.
rca of the issue: No blocking issues identified. All feature and regression tests are green.
